result:
Build is green. No instrumentation was added (didn't need any — the bug was localizable from the test source + the code).

**Root cause:** `unpackint` in `crates/lua-stdlib/src/string_lib.rs:1825-1836` had a broken signed-extension translation. For `unpack("b", "\x80")` with `res=0x80, mask=0x80`, the first transformation block (a contorted misread of C's `(res ^ mask) - mask`) corrupted `res` to `0x100`, then the second fallback check (`res & 0x80 != 0`) was false, so no sign extension happened — returning `256` instead of `-128`. This made tpack.lua line 47 (`assert(unpack("b", pack("b", -0x80)) == -0x80)`) fail.

**Fix:** Replaced the broken two-stage logic with the canonical C-Lua formula `res = (res ^ mask).wrapping_sub(mask)` — a single wrapping op that correctly sign-extends any `size < SZINT` two's-complement value.

**New state:** Did not re-run; the mega-loop will re-test. Compile is green. Expected: `tpack.lua` either passes or reveals a later, unrelated failure.

tool_input.command: cargo build -p lua-cli 2>/tmp/build.err; grep -B 2 -A 10 "no method named" /tmp/build.err | head -40
